faq_a1: The GIF format and the Minecraft rendering engine do NOT support semi-transparent pixels. Pixels will be forced to either 0% or 100% opacity upon export. To avoid glitches, always place an opaque layer (like a solid color) under your semi-transparent textures.
faq_a2:
WynntilsMinecraft Capes
Free Max Size128×642048×1024
Paid PlansDonator: Max 512×256, AnimatedPremium: Animated
File/Frame Limit≤ 500 KB≤ 500 KB / Max 64 frames
Animation PlaybackPlays all frames within 2sPlays 1 frame every 100ms
Export FormatPNG (Static) / PNG Sprite (Animated)PNG (Static) / GIF (Animated)
faq_q3: 3. Can I make static capes?▼
faq_q4: 4. Does it support Cosmetica animated capes?▼
faq_a4: Yes. Cosmetica supports both GIF animations and PNG Sprite sheets (similar to Wynntils). You can choose either export mode. Note that playback speed is handled within the Cosmetica system, and there is a maximum limit of 20 frames.
faq_q5: 5. What import formats are supported?▼
faq_a5: Static images, GIF, and APNG are supported. APNG is recommended to preserve more details and avoid secondary quality loss during export.
faq_q6: 6. Animation speed looks too fast or too slow?▼
faq_a6:
Wynntils: Fixed to play all frames within 2 seconds.

  • If the original animation is shorter than 2s, the frame duration will be stretched, making it look slower. Use the "Speed Multiplier" to speed it up (copies frames to loop multiple times in 2s).
  • If the original animation is longer than 2s, frames will be compressed, making it look faster. It's recommended to crop to 2s before importing.
  • ⚠ If the original duration doesn't divide evenly into 2s, the speed may be slightly off.

Minecraft Capes: Fixed to play 1 frame every 100ms.

  • If the original frame delay is < 100ms, playback will look slower.
  • If the original frame delay is > 100ms, playback will look faster.
  • In MinecraftCapes mode, enabling "Resample" samples frames at 100ms intervals to ensure consistent total duration. However, for extremely short and fast animations, this may cause choppy or static playback. Disabling it preserves all frames (though in-game playback might be slower). For best results, preprocess your animation to 10fps (100ms/frame) using FFmpeg before importing.
faq_q7: 7. Can I use this on a mobile phone?▼
faq_a7: Yes. The interface automatically adapts to mobile phones. However, operation is slightly different, and some features are simplified (e.g., no keyboard shortcuts). For precise editing, using a computer is recommended.
faq_q8: 8. Can I draw my own cape here?▼
faq_a8: No. This tool is designed for quick collage. If you need to draw, use tools like SkinMC.
faq_q9: 9. Does it support frame-by-frame editing?▼
faq_a9: No. It's recommended to prepare your assets in professional software like Photoshop before importing.
confirm_project_load: Importing a project will overwrite your current work. Continue?
confirm_page_leave: You have unsaved changes. Are you sure you want to leave?
opacity_transparency_tip: ⚠ Semi-transparency is NOT supported by GIF or MC engine. Ensure an opaque layer is underneath.
resample_desc: Resamples frames at 100ms intervals to match MinecraftCapes. Note: if the original animation is extremely short and fast, resampling might cause it to become discontinuous or even stop entirely. Disabling preserves all original frames, but in-game speed may differ.
target_fps_desc: Adjusts the base framerate of the animation. Final exported FPS will scale dynamically along with your speed multiplier.
target_fps_unsupported: Framerate adjustment is disabled because the original animation is 10 FPS or below.
playback_duration: Wynntils animation cycle is fixed at 2s. Higher multiplier = more loops per 2s (Faster).
minecraftcapes_playback_info: MinecraftCapes plays 1 frame per 100ms. Changing the multiplier resamples the frames to change the speed (e.g., 0.5x repeats frames, 2x skips frames). Capes are exported as GIFs.
oxipng_desc: Lossless compression. Reduces file size without losing any quality (takes more time).
upng_desc: Lossy compression. Converts image to 256 colors. Greatly reduces file size and saves memory in Minecraft, with minor quality loss.
gif_quality_desc: Higher values reduce color sampling accuracy. (1 = Best Quality, 20 = Lowest Quality)
auto_select_tip: Auto-select clicked layers (Disable to lock selection)
alert_only_one_gif: Only one animated GIF/APNG per project. Duplicate existing animated layers instead.
confirm_reset: Are you sure you want to reset to default configuration? This cannot be undone, current work state will be reset.
tour_welcome_desc: This tutorial will guide you through the features of the online animated cape creator.
tour_project_desc: You can Save your current work as an .anicape file and Load it back later. This preserves all your layers, animations, and custom regions.
tour_workspace_desc: This is your main canvas. You can drag, resize, and position your uploaded cape layers here.
tour_template_desc: Choose your cape resolution (such as standard 128x64 or HD sizes) and select the Target Mod to configure animated exporting behavior.
tour_advanced_desc: Toggle this to customize region colors like Front, Back, Elytra, and set color tolerance for auto-clipping.
tour_layers_desc: Upload images or animated (APNG/GIF) here. They will appear on the canvas where you can manage their stacking order.
tour_properties_desc: When a layer is selected, you can adjust its opacity, rotation, and clip it to specific template regions.
tour_history_desc: Use these to undo or redo your actions if you make a mistake.
tour_zoom_desc: Use these buttons or your mouse wheel to zoom in/out or center the canvas view.
tour_export_desc: Click here to process your layers into a PNG.
tour_export_modal_desc: Here you can reduce colors (8-bit) to drastically cut size, or use OxiPNG for lossless compression. Click Export and then Download when finished.
tour_drag_drop_desc: You can easily drag and drop your image directly onto the workspace to add a layer, or drag an .anicape file to quickly open a project!
tour_shortcuts_desc: Learning these shortcuts is crucial for working efficiently! They will save you a lot of time switching tools.
tour_mobile_desc: On mobile devices, open the sidebar using the menu button, and move layers using directional arrows.
tour_end_desc: Enjoy creating your custom animated capes. You can replay this tutorial anytime from the top-right button.
about_intro: AniCape is a pure frontend online tool designed for Minecraft players to create animated capes for Wynntils and MinecraftCapes. The entire application runs directly in your browser without any backend server. Your images never leave your device.
about_origin_p2: This tool was born out of extreme frustration with "frame-by-frame animation processing". Previously, to generate a PNG Sprite compliant with mod specifications, creators had to repeat tedious actions in Photoshop: disassembling frames one by one, arranging them grid by grid, and performing pixel-perfect alignment. Such trivial and inefficient mechanical labor often grinds down creative passion.
about_origin_p3: Thus, AniCape was born — aiming to end these meaningless operations. Now you just simply paste a GIF or APNG, and the remaining tedious layout and format conversions are entirely handled automatically by the program.
about_origin_p4: "We believe creators' time should be wasted on creativity itself, not on repetitive operations."
about_feat_layers: Layer System — Supports multi-layer stacking, drag-and-drop sorting, flipping, rotation, scaling, and opacity adjustment
about_feat_anim: Animation Support — Import GIF/APNG animations, auto-split into frames with real-time preview
about_feat_clip: Area Clipping — Automatically identifying Front, Back, Elytra areas based on the template color map, with custom tolerance support
about_feat_export: Dual Export Modes — Wynntils (PNG Sprite) and MinecraftCapes (GIF Animation) outputs
about_feat_compress: Compression Tuning — Built-in OxiPNG lossless compression + UPNG.js 8-bit color quantization for minimal file size
about_feat_3d: 3D Preview — Real-time preview of Cape/Elytra effect on a 3D character model before exporting
about_feat_undo: Undo / Redo — Complete history stack with up to 50 undo steps
about_feat_rwd: RWD Responsive — Touch operation adapted for mobile and tablet devices
about_feat_i18n: Bilingual i18n — Supports Traditional Chinese / English switching
about_arch_desc: The project uses pure HTML/CSS/JavaScript (No Frameworks), modularized into the following files:
about_arch_core: src/core/ — EventBus (pub/sub), StateManager (validated state writes), global state, localization (i18n), and utility functions.
about_arch_logic: src/logic/ — Business logic: history stack (Undo/Redo), rendering engine, animation playback, layer CRUD, and project saving.
about_arch_layers: src/layers/ — LayerTransform: single source of truth for scale, rotation, and hit-testing helpers.
about_arch_ui_mod: src/ui/ — Visual components for the layer list, property panels, and color region editors.
about_arch_parsers: src/parsers/ — Specialized handlers for binary GIF decoding and APNG/GIF frame extraction.
about_arch_export_mod: src/export/ — Image compositor, optimization (OxiPNG/UPNG), and 3D preview engine.
about_arch_events_mod: src/events/ — Pure event modules handling dragging, zooming, keyboard shortcuts, and touch interactions.
about_arch_workers: src/workers/ — Web Workers for off-thread PNG encoding (UPNG) and lossless compression (OxiPNG/WASM).
about_libs_pako: High-performance zlib compression/decompression, used internally by UPNG.js (Inflate/Deflate)
about_libs_upng: PNG/APNG decoder and encoder. Supports APNG frame extraction and 8-bit color quantization
about_libs_gif: GIF encoder running in a Web Worker to generate GIFs, used for MinecraftCapes animation export
about_libs_omggif: GIF decoder, reads GIF binary data and renders frames to Canvas (with dispose method handling)
about_libs_oxipng: Rust-compiled WebAssembly lossless PNG compressor, provides 0-6 compression levels
about_libs_jszip: Archive and extract .anicape project files (ZIP format), managing layer images and JSON metadata
about_libs_skinview3d: Three.js-based Minecraft character 3D renderer, used for exporting preview of Cape/Elytra
about_libs_driver: Lightweight interactive tutorial tool, used to build the Step-by-step onboarding guide
about_footer: This tool is completely open-source and free to use under the MIT License. All image processing runs locally in your browser and is never uploaded to any server.
